claim: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 100 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%.

corpus.title: Dose Escalation Study Report
corpus.snippet: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%....
corpus.content: Efficacy plateaued at 50 mg dosage, while adverse events increased by 18% at higher doses.

corpus.title: News: MedCore Research Group — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased ad...
corpus.content:
MedCore Research Group ANNOUNCES KEY SCIENTIFIC UPDATE

A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. The announcement comes as changes in the scientific sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.content:
MedCore Research Group ANNOUNCES SHOCKING SCIENTIFIC REVELATION

A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 100 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. The announcement comes as growing scrutiny in the scientific sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.
